name: Путь Гневных Мертвецов
description: Barbarians who walk the Path of the Wrathful Dead commune with the spirits of the deceased and channel their spectral powers. These Barbarians are often preoccupied with the dead whispering in their ears, which urge them to act upon the whims and emotions of the haunting spirits. Some who walk this path learn to master the powers gifted to them; others find they've become servants to the unending chorus clamoring for their attention.
shortDescription: Channel the Rage of the Unquiet Dead

features.name: Rage of the Dead
features.description: Your Rage taps into the endless fury of the unquiet dead. While your Rage is active, you take on aspects of restless spirits and you gain the following benefits.

details.shadowForm: You ignore {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. In addition, you can move through the space of any creature, but you can't end your move in an occupied space.
details.shadowySidestep: Your {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 10 feet, and {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} against you.
details.spectralSight: You see creatures and objects within 120 feet that have the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition as if they were visible, and you can see into the Ethereal Plane.

features.name: Final Night Catharsis
features.description: You are overcome by an emotion a nearby spirit experienced at its death. You gain one of the following options of your choice. Whenever you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, you can change your choice.

details.hate: While your Rage is active, your attacks with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
details.jealousy: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to summon spectral assailants to {@condition Grapple|XPHB} the creature. The creature makes a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failure, the creature has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition until the end of its turn. On a successful save, the creature is not {@condition Grappled|XPHB}; however, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot for that creature until the end of its turn.
details.terror: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make that creature terrified until the start of its next turn. A terrified creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved and {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.

features.name: Dark Doom Revisited
features.description: Once per active Rage, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to channel a traumatic death. When you do, choose one of the following options.

details.contamination: At the start of their turn, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or take Poison damage and gain the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute. To determine the Poison damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. An affected creature can use an action to end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition on itself.
details.hypothermia: At the start of their turn, each creature of your choice in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 8 plus your Strength modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or take Cold damage and have its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} halved for 1 minute. To determine the Cold damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. A creature can use an action to end this effect on itself.
details.immolation: At the start of their turn, each creature of your choice in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation|XPHB} originating from you must make a Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}) or take Fire damage. To determine the Fire damage, roll a number of {@dice d6}s equal to your Rage Damage bonus, and add them together. A creature can extinguish the fire on itself by giving itself the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and rolling on the ground. The fire also goes out if it is doused, submerged, or suffocated.

features.name: Death Is but a Door
features.description: Your familiarity with death has left you resistant to its call. You gain the following benefits.

details.hardToKill: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throws|XPHB}. In addition, you must fail four {@variantrule Death Saving Throws|XPHB} to die instead of three as normal.
details.returnTheSpirit: You can call the spirits of the deceased to restore life essence to a nearby creature. You can cast the {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB}, or {@spell Revivify|XPHB} spell without providing Material components. When you do, you gain 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level for {@spell Cure Wounds|XPHB}, 2 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels for {@spell Revivify|XPHB}, and 3 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels for {@spell Raise Dead|XPHB}. Constitution is your spellcasting ability for this feature. Once you use this feature, you can't use it again until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} unless you expend two uses of your Rage (no action required) to restore your use of it.

features.name: Powered by Pathos
features.description: Your Rage is empowered by the overwhelming emotions of the unquiet dead. Your Final Night Catharsis feature grants an additional effect based on the chosen emotion.

details.hate: While your Rage is active, your attacks with weapons and {@variantrule Unarmed Strikes|XPHB} score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}.
details.jealousy: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to summon spectral assailants to {@condition Grapple|XPHB} the creature. The creature makes a Strength or Dexterity saving throw (DC 8 plus your Constitution modifier and {@variantrule Proficiency Bonus|XPHB}). On a failure, the creature has the {@condition Grappled|XPHB} condition until the end of its turn. On a successful save, the creature is not {@condition Grappled|XPHB}; however, each foot of movement costs 1 extra foot for that creature until the end of its turn.
details.terror: Whenever a creature you can see starts its turn within 30 feet of you while your Rage is active, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to make that creature terrified until the start of its next turn. A terrified creature's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved and {@variantrule Opportunity Attacks|XPHB} against it have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
